title: Tip: Use Pop Dots as Embellishments
excerpt: Love taking something I have had for years and adding new life to it. If you're a paper crafter, you've probably stocked up on plenty of pop dots to add dimension to your projects. Instead of using them traditionally, I…
body:
Love taking something I have had for years and adding new life to it. If you're a paper crafter, you've probably stocked up on plenty of pop dots to add dimension to your projects. Instead of using them traditionally, I took the utility out and make them polka dots. Instead of peeling both sides for the adhesive, I only did one and kept the paper backing on the exposed side.

1979 // Copyright Two Peas in A Bucket, where there is a complete list of supplies used, except for white Zing! which can be found at Studio Calico. // Two Peas In A Bucket 

FREE shipping Code for August is: H3QJ4R Instead of using the original photo, I took a picture of it using my iPhone and uploaded it thru the free Lifepics app. Then when I got onto my computer, they automatically appeared in my Persnickety Prints account, where I chose the sizes for my order. The quality of the photos from Persnickety is fantastic. This particular print is 3x3" on cardstock, one of my favorites for Instagram printing. This might be the way I reprint some of my favorite old photos - easy and compliments the vintage look. All makes for a fresh twist on oldies but goodies.
